topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The click of the safe deposit box opening echoed in the silence of the study. I stared at the ledger, the numbers swimming before my eyes. For months, I’d built this life with him, meticulously planning, sharing every ambition. And tucked inside, hidden behind a false panel, was a separate account, substantial. A secret hoard. A cold knot tightened in my chest, but I didn't flinch. I picked up the pen, my knuckles white against the paper, and began to make notes, cataloging the sheer audacity of it all. He’d built this fortress of silence, and now I held the key. A slow, almost predatory smile touched my lips.

[story 2]

I found the access code tucked inside an old library book, a childish little trick. When the digital screen finally illuminated, the balance made my breath catch. It wasn't just money; it was a whole other world, built in shadow while I was busy polishing the living room. A surge of hot anger shot through me, sharp and exhilarating. I didn't feel guilt; I felt a sudden, profound sense of power. I closed the laptop with a decisive snap, the sound slicing through the quiet apartment. I looked at him across the dinner table, his expression placid, utterly unaware of the seismic shift happening beneath the surface. I decided, right then, that I was done being the quiet supporter.

[story 3]

The paper felt crisp, almost illicit in my hand. Every digit represented a choice he hadn't made with me. I walked to the window, the city lights blurring into streaks of indifferent color. My jaw was tight, a hard line set beneath my skin. It was ridiculous, this discovery, but the feeling inside was pure, unadulterated refusal. I didn't want an explanation; I didn't want an apology. I simply wanted to examine the evidence, to understand the architecture of his hidden life. With a deliberate movement, I slipped the documents into my coat pocket, the weight a solid anchor against the uncertainty of the evening. I felt lighter, strangely unbound.
